subject: Configure Supabase Auth with Google OAuth
description: Create docs/SUPABASE_SETUP.md with complete instructions: 1) Create Supabase project, 2) Enable Google OAuth provider in Authentication settings, 3) Configure Google Cloud Console OAuth credentials (create project, create OAuth 2.0 credentials for Web application, set authorized redirect URIs for web and mobile). NOTE: Google+ API is DEPRECATED - do NOT reference it. For basic OAuth, no additional APIs need to be enabled. If you need profile data beyond email, enable 'Google People API' instead. 4) Set redirect URIs in Supabase (https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1/callback), 5) Configure site URL and redirect URLs. Create supabase/config.toml with local development settings. Create .env.example with SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET placeholders. Document mobile deep link schemes (worktracker://) for OAuth redirect.
result_summary: Added Supabase/Google OAuth setup documentation and local auth configuration. Updated worktracker/.env.example with GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET placeholders plus deep-link guidance, added worktracker/supabase/config.toml with local Supabase Auth settings for Google (`client_id`/`secret` from env, local site URL, and redirect allow-list including `worktracker://auth/callback`), and created worktracker/docs/SUPABASE_SETUP.md explaining the full setup flow across Google Cloud Console, Supabase Dashboard, and local Supabase CLI. Recorded an auth decision clarifying that Google redirects only to Supabase `/auth/v1/callback`, while app web/native callbacks belong in Supabase URL configuration. Validation: `npm run typecheck` passed, `npx prettier --check docs/SUPABASE_SETUP.md` passed, and `git diff --check` passed for the touched files. `git pull --rebase` could not be completed because the shared worktree already had unrelated unstaged changes and no configured remote. Committed as `4f84345` with message `[task-010] Document Supabase Google OAuth setup`. Tests beyond typecheck were not applicable because this task only added configuration/docs and the repo does not yet expose a dedicated automated auth test suite.
